sourceMovie.title: But I'm a Cheerleader
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit But I'm a Cheerleader for the same reason it broke through in 2000: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a romance rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jamie Babbit keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Love, Simon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If But I'm a Cheerleader clicked for you, Love, Simon (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after But I'm a Cheerleader?
faq.answer: Try Love, Simon, Do Revenge, Can't Buy Me Love—each shares DNA with But I'm a Cheerleader while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is But I'm a Cheerleader?
faq.answer: But I'm a Cheerleader (2000) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is But I'm a Cheerleader worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
